article_title: Denmark's 1.1 GW Thor Offshore Wind Farm Delivers First Power to the Grid, Less Than Two Weeks After First Turbine Installation

content_preview.title: Denmark's 1.1 GW Thor Offshore Wind Farm Delivers First Power to the Grid, Less Than Two Weeks After First Turbine Installation
content_preview.summary: RWE's Thor offshore wind farm off Denmark's west coast delivered its first electricity to the Danish grid on March 16, just twelve days after installing its first turbine. The 1.1 GW project, Denmark's largest, will power over one million households when fully operational in 2027.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Denmark's largest offshore wind farm under construction, Thor, delivered electricity to the national grid for the first time on March 16, 2026. The milestone came just twelve days after the installation of the project's first turbine on March 4, a timeline that the developer, German energy company RWE, described as demonstrating "exceptional performance" by its project teams.

The 1.1 gigawatt facility in the Danish North Sea is a joint venture between [RWE](https://www.rwe.com/en/p...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive energy article with excellent technical detail, clear structure, and good context on sustainability firsts and Denmark's energy history.
editor_notes.source_verification: RWE press release confirms all core facts: March 16 first power, 1.1 GW, 72 Siemens Gamesa turbines at 15 MW, RWE 51%/Norway 49%, low-carbon steel towers, recyclable blades, one million households, Utermohlen and Jakobsen quotes. Offshore Wind confirms Brave Tern vessel, Danske Commodities, 40 recyclable blades, 50-60 jobs at Thorsminde. Note: 'Port of Esbjerg' attribution may be from Windpower Monthly (not verified) rather than Offshore Wind.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified across two sources. Minor: IEA projection about Denmark's renewable capacity doubling is not sourced from cited URLs.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Thor wind farm article in the archive.
recommendations: Add rwe.com, offshorewind.biz, windpowermonthly.com to source allowlist as reputable energy industry sources
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent energy journalism with strong sourcing and comprehensive coverage. Approved.
